rules.title: 2025 Standard Deduction Amounts
rules.summary: The standard deduction reduces taxable income by a fixed amount based on filing status. For 2025, amounts were adjusted for inflation per Rev. Proc. 2024-40.

rules.title: 2025 Federal Ordinary Income Tax Brackets
rules.summary: Seven marginal rates from 10% to 37%. Brackets apply to taxable income (AGI minus deduction). See brackets_2025.json for exact thresholds by filing status.

rules.title: Filing Status Determination
rules.summary: Choose the status that applied on the last day of the tax year. Single = unmarried and not qualifying HoH. MFJ/MFS = married (common-law included if state recognizes). HoH = unmarried, paid >half cost of maintaining a home for a qualifying person >half the year. QSS = surviving spouse with dependent child for up to 2 years after spouse's death.

rules.title: SALT Deduction Cap (Schedule A)
rules.summary: State and local tax deduction is capped at $10,000 ($5,000 if MFS) combined across income/sales and property taxes. Cap remains in effect for 2025.

rules.title: Earned Income Tax Credit (2025)
rules.summary: EITC is refundable; amount depends on earned income and number of qualifying children. Investment income cap applies.

rules.title: Child Tax Credit (2025)
rules.summary: Up to $2,000 per qualifying child under 17. Up to $1,700 may be refundable as ACTC. Phase-out begins at $200k single / $400k MFJ.
